patches.hypothesis: The method addValue is casting Object to Comparable without checking if the object is actually an instance of Comparable, leading to a ClassCastException.
patches.original_code:
/** * Adds 1 to the frequency count for v. *

* If other objects have already been added to this Frequency, v must * be comparable to those that have already been added. *

* * @param v the value to add. * @throws IllegalArgumentException if v is not Comparable, * or is not comparable with previous entries * @deprecated use {@link #addValue(Comparable)} instead */ public void addValue(Object v) { addValue((Comparable) v); }

patches.hypothesis: The test fails when adding a non-comparable object, causing a ClassCastException. We should check if the object is an instance of Comparable and throw an IllegalArgumentException if not.
patches.changes: Added check: if (!(v instanceof Comparable<?>)) throw new IllegalArgumentException();
patches.diff: Added check: if (!(v instanceof Comparable<?>)) throw new IllegalArgumentException();

patches.hypothesis: The method addValue is incorrectly attempting to cast any Object to Comparable without checking its type first. This causes a ClassCastException when the object is not actually a Comparable.
patches.changes: - Add an instanceof check to ensure Object v is a Comparable before casting.
patches.diff: - Add an instanceof check to ensure Object v is a Comparable before casting.
